title: TagInput
description: Tag editor: the user types text, presses Enter (or a delimiter) and a chip is added. Supports validation, maximums and visual variants.

playground.placeholder: Add a tag and press Enter…

basic.title: Basic usage
basic.desc: Press Enter or comma to confirm. Duplicates are discarded by default.
basic.ph: Add a tag and press Enter…

max.title: Maximum tags
max.desc: Limit the number of tags with the max prop.

validator.title: Custom validator
validator.desc: Only tags that pass the validator are confirmed (here, valid emails).

sizes.title: Sizes
sizes.desc: Three heights to match the form density.

props.placeholder: Placeholder when there are no tags and the input is empty.
props.max: Maximum number of tags. Beyond the limit the input is disabled.
props.validator: Optional validator. Return false to reject the tag.
props.delimiters: Characters that also confirm the current tag when typed.
props.size: Size of the input and chips.
props.radius: Corner radius of the wrapper and chips.
props.readonly: Read-only mode — tags cannot be removed.

emits.updateModelValue: Emitted when the tag list changes (v-model).
emits.add: Emitted when a new tag is added.
emits.remove: Emitted when a tag is removed, with its value and original index.
emits.change: Emitted after any change (add or remove).

types.title: TagInput
types.desc: TypeScript interface for the TagInput component props. Supports custom validators, delimiters, duplicate prevention, and chip theming.

a11y.kbTab: Move focus to the next focusable element
a11y.kbShiftTab: Move focus to the previous focusable element
a11y.kbType: Type a tag name and press Enter or a delimiter to add it
a11y.kbBackspace: Remove the last tag when the input is empty
a11y.ariaInvalid: Set to true when errorText is present
a11y.ariaDescribedBy: Links helper or error text to the input for screen reader context
a11y.ariaDisabled: Communicates disabled state to assistive technologies
a11y.ariaLabel: Provides an accessible name when no visible label is present
a11y.focusNative: Uses a native input element — participates in the tab order by default
a11y.focusChips: Each tag chip has a remove button that is focusable and keyboard-activatable
